reviewer: agent build+matrix pass (runnability verified end-to-end on the page: real metric depth rendered; desktop+mobile screenshots read; light+dark checked), revision 1

rubric.evidence: RUNNABILITY VERIFIED end-to-end (headless Chrome, WASM path): transformers.js 3.7.5 registers Metric3DForDepthEstimation; pipeline('depth-estimation','onnx-community/metric3d-vit-small') loaded and produced a real result. On the page, clicking Estimate rendered a colourised depth map (depthCanvas width=512) and a metric readout with ZERO console errors and ZERO failed network requests. screenshots: reports/responsive/screens/metric3d-depth/desktop.png, reports/responsive/screens/metric3d-depth/mobile.png
rubric.notes: Inference + the dense colour composite both run in a Web Worker; the worker transfers a ready ImageBitmap (invariant 15). Backend was WASM·fp32 on this no-GPU runner; WebGPU·fp16 is used when an adapter exists.

rubric.evidence: REAL OUTPUT observed: predicted_depth dims [512 × 386], metric range 2.42 – 7.37 m, mean 5.400 m for the bundled room scene — physically plausible absolute-scale (metre) depth, DISTINCT from a relative 0–1 map. The see-inside table showed the raw metre range + mean + near/far planes derived from the model's own predicted_depth tensor.
rubric.notes: Metric (absolute) output is the defining difference vs the built Depth Anything / DPT (relative) demos; the metres are calibrated to the model's canonical camera intrinsics (surfaced as a caveat in the UI).

rubric.evidence: At-a-glance declares the exact model id (onnx-community/metric3d-vit-small, linked), task (depth-estimation), backend (WebGPU/WASM) and dtype (fp16/fp32). The readout showed 'WASM · fp32' from the real run. Inference is off the main thread (new Worker + worker.js).

The page loads through the shared createModelLoader auto-init architecture (no hand-rolled Load button). First visit shows Download (~72 MB); after a validated load the cache/version layer records it. Verified the loader states drive correctly with no console errors.
rubric.notes: Auto-init-on-cached is the shared model-loader/model-cache behaviour (same as every built demo); a returning user with a validated cache initialises without a Download click.

rubric.evidence: Loading/ready state is announced in role=status aria-live regions; a Retry path exists on failure; download progress is shown. On this runner the model downloaded and the run succeeded; the status line reported 'Done.' with no console error.
rubric.notes: Errors surface on the page (status .err), not only the console.

rubric.evidence: Every control is real and keyboard-operable: sample thumbnails (Enter/Space), drop-zone file upload, colour-map select (44px target), Estimate button. The colour-map change re-colourises OFF the main thread (worker recolor → transferred ImageBitmap). Verified the Estimate click produced output.
rubric.notes: The practical page adds a tap-to-measure canvas reading real metres; the wild page a rAF-bounded 3D parallax; the multi-model page composes Metric3D × Segformer.

rubric.evidence: No canned output anywhere — the worker runs the real transformers.js depth-estimation pipeline and derives the depth image, metric range, mean, histogram and per-pixel metre field from its output. The rendered 2.42–7.37 m range is straight from predicted_depth.

All images carry alt text; all buttons have accessible names; canvases carry aria-labels; the colour legend labels near/far with metre values. Verified light+dark render legibly (WCAG-AA design system).

AGENT-VERIFIED: read desktop (1280×800) and mobile (360×740, DPR3) screenshots — measured 0px horizontal overflow on mobile, no clipped controls, legible text, links wrap. The run-it grid, canvas cards and ladder columns use flex-wrap with min-inline-size:0; canvases scale to inline-size:100% with a max-block-size cap.
rubric.notes: Usable on BOTH classes; the model runs on WASM so no device class is excluded.

rubric.evidence: AGENT-VERIFIED: inference AND the per-pixel colour composite run in a Web Worker (invariant 15) — the main thread only does a single drawImage of a transferred ImageBitmap. The wild page's parallax warp is rAF-bounded, capped-resolution, pointer-gated and cancels on pagehide. No blocking loop observed on load; console stayed clean.
rubric.notes: Dense-output composite moved off the main thread per the measured systemic hotspot in invariant 15.

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive control panel and canvas/image layout without horizontal overflow
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Fluid flex/grid with flex-wrap + min-inline-size:0 and intrinsic sizing; media inline-size:100% + aspect-ratio; logical properties; avoid fixed widths — see §2 Flexbox and §6 overflow tracking.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the run-it grid, two-up ladder columns and canvas cards use flex-wrap with min-inline-size:0; canvases cap at max-block-size and scale to 100%. Measured 0px horizontal overflow at 360px.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: responsive matrix desktop+mobile; agent read screenshots; 0px overflow

guidanceConsulted.query: offscreen canvas dense image composite in a web worker without blocking the main thread
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Move heavy per-pixel work off the main thread; keep any main-thread loop bounded / yield so it doesn't spike INP.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the colour-map composite runs on an OffscreenCanvas in the worker and is transferred back as an ImageBitmap (single main-thread drawImage); inference is in the worker; the parallax warp is rAF-bounded and pointer-gated.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: worker.js colourise() + transferToImageBitmap; wild/index.html rAF warp; console clean

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible live region status updates for loading progress
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce loading/ready/error via role=status aria-live; label every control and canvas; keyboard-operable samples.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the shared loader + page status use role=status aria-live; sample thumbs handle Enter/Space; canvases and legend carry aria-labels.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: buttons-have-names + images-have-alt conformance pass; live-status-region present

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use cross-document view transitions for catalogue↔page navigation.

openQuestions: A real WebGPU-device pass (fp16, ~72 MB) would confirm the faster path + fp16 numerical parity with the WASM fp32 metres measured here.

Metric3D's absolute metres are calibrated to its canonical camera intrinsics; a follow-up could expose a focal-length input to refine absolute scale for user photos (the relative structure is already robust).

followUpGoals.goal: WebGPU-device pass for metric3d-depth: run on a real WebGPU machine, confirm the fp16 path renders the same metric range + colourised map and enrich this critique with the real WebGPU latency.
Add an optional camera-intrinsics / focal-length control to refine absolute-metre calibration for arbitrary user photos, documenting the effect on the measured range.

summary: Revision-1 build+matrix pass for metric3d-depth (onnx-community/metric3d-vit-small, depth-estimation, model_type metric3d — absolute-scale MONOCULAR depth in metres, DISTINCT from the built Depth Anything / DPT relative maps and from the large WebGPU-only DepthPro). RUNNABILITY VERIFIED end-to-end: transformers.js 3.7.5 registers Metric3DForDepthEstimation and the page rendered a real colourised depth map with a metric range of 2.42–7.37 m (mean 5.400 m, predicted_depth [512×386]) on the WASM path, ZERO console errors, ZERO failed requests, 0px mobile overflow, desktop+mobile screenshots read. Inference AND the dense colour composite run off the main thread with a transferred ImageBitmap (invariant 15). 14/23 assertions auto-pass, remainder manual-evidenced above; real output + full parity confirmed. Runs on WASM so no device class is excluded.
